reasoning: The resolution requires more than 10 times the baseline value of 'Explosions/Remote violence' events in a 30-day window. The baseline is 1.000, so this requires at least 11 events in 30 days. Guinea-Bissau had a military coup in late November 2025, but it did not devolve into a sustained armed conflict or insurgency involving heavy weapons, airstrikes, or widespread IEDs. News searches confirm absolutely zero remote violence events in Guinea-Bissau throughout January 2026, meaning the rate is currently at 0. Reaching 11 events in either the Jan 9 - Feb 8 window or the Feb 1 - Mar 3 window would require a sudden outbreak of intense warfare (like what is seen in Burkina Faso or Sudan), which is unprecedented in modern Guinea-Bissau post-coup environments. The probability is effectively zero, so I set the forecast to the lowest allowable bounds (0.05 and 0.06) strictly to respect the 5% minimum probability rule while showing slight uncertainty increase over time.
